name: Route of the Castles: Villena - Sax Loop
name_original: Tour por el Alto Vinalopó – Circular desde Villena hasta Sax
description: This easy 19.3-mile touring cycling route from Villena to Sax offers a comfortable ride through diverse landscapes and past historical sites
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villena traverses a diverse landscape characterized by rocky and limestone ecosystems, blending Meseta and Mediterranean features. The region features prominent mountain ranges like Sierra de Salinas and Sierra de la Villa, offering varied terrain. Cyclists can explore unique environments such as the rounded hills of Los Cabezos, the Vinalopó River valley, and ancient salt flats. Pine forests and peaceful valleys also contribute to the varied scenery available for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Villena The most popular touring cycling route is Route of the Castles: Villena - Sax Loop, a 19.3 miles (31.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This easy route combines diverse landscapes with historical sites. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Atalaya Castle – Cave House Ruins of Villena loop from Villena, a difficult 23.4 miles (37.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and passes by historical landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Colonia de Santa Eulalia – Villena loop from Villena, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with substantial climbs, often completed in about 4 hours 29 minutes. Touring cycling around Villena is defined by mountain ranges, the Vinalopó River valley, and ancient salt flats. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy Vías Verdes to challenging climbs. The routes in Villena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 2400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villena?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes around Villena, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored more than 118 routes in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Villena?
A: Villena's landscape is incredibly diverse, blending rocky and limestone ecosystems with both Meseta and Mediterranean characteristics. You'll encounter prominent mountain ranges like the Sierra de Salinas and Sierra de la Villa, offering challenging climbs and rewarding descents. There are also unique environments such as the rounded hills of Los Cabezos, the Vinalopó River valley, ancient salt flats, and peaceful pine forests and olive groves, ensuring varied scenery on every ride.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Villena?
A: Villena offers routes for all abilities. You'll find 14 easy routes, 49 moderate routes, and 55 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both casual riders and experienced cyclists can find suitable challenges.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Villena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Villena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 2400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villena's varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the mix of historical and natural attractions.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly touring cycling routes in Villena?
A: Yes, Villena offers several easy routes, perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952681204/route-of-the-castles-villena-sax-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Castles: Villena - Sax Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 19.3-mile (31 km) trail that combines diverse landscapes with historical sites. Additionally, the Vía Verde del Chicharra, a disused railway line converted into a cycling path, provides mostly flat and traffic-free routes ideal for family outings.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant climbs in Villena?
A: Absolutely. Villena is home to many difficult routes with substantial elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31917800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Castle – Cave House Ruins of Villena loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 23.4-mile (37.7 km) path featuring over 640 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31931436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biar – Villena loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;, which also includes significant climbs.

Q: Are there any long-distance or multi-day touring cycling options from Villena?
A: Yes, Villena is part of larger cycling networks that offer long-distance possibilities. The Vía Verde del Chicharra can take cyclists towards Gandía, offering approximately 80 km of dedicated path and the opportunity to connect with the Vía Verde de Villalonga for a route of around 100 km that descends to sea level. Villena is also featured on sections of the historic El Camino del Cid and the Ruta de la Lana, both suitable for gravel biking and longer tours.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Villena?
A: Many routes around Villena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952681204/route-of-the-castles-villena-sax-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Castles: Villena - Sax Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31917800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Castle – Cave House Ruins of Villena loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14644937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colonia de Santa Eulalia – Villena loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I visit while cycling in Villena?
A: Villena is rich in history. You can cycle to the imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5128081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a 12th-century Almohad fortress offering spectacular panoramic views. Other historical sites accessible by bike include the Church of Santiago, known for its Levantine Gothic architecture, and the archaeological site of Cabezo Redondo, home to Bronze Age remains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5900085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Sax&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable landmark on some routes.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on Villena's cycling routes?
A: Yes, Villena's routes offer numerous scenic spots. You can enjoy panoramic views from the Sierra de la Villa (or San Cristobal) and from the Atalaya Castle. The region's unique natural features include the Vinalopó River valley, ancient salt flats with their circuit of salt basins, and the distinctive rounded hills of Los Cabezos. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6668923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Reconco from Alt de la Replana&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3867106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Elda from El Bolón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Villena?
A: Villena's climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring. Summers can be hot, especially in inland areas, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are mild, making it a good option for those looking to escape colder climates.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Vías Verdes (Greenways) in Villena?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Chicharra is a significant draw for touring cyclists in Villena. This disused railway line has been converted into a cycling path, providing a mostly flat and traffic-free route that is ideal for relaxed touring and connecting to other areas like Gandía.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Villena?
A: Villena, as a town, offers various parking options. For specific routes, it's often best to look for parking near the starting points of the trails or in the town center, which provides good access to many routes. Many routes are designed as loops, making it convenient to park your vehicle and return to it after your ride.
tips:
The oldest part of the castle is the eastern tower, built of tapia masonry on the steep slope of the cliff. It dates from the Almohad period, i.e., the late 12th to early 13th century. The second tower of the complex probably dates from the first decades of the 14th century. Originally a Moorish foundation, after the Reconquista the castle was located in the border area between the Kingdom of Murcia, claimed by Castile, and the Kingdom of Valencia, conquered by Aragon. A chain of castles was built in this area, mainly along the Vinalopó River. Around 1240, the castle was wrested from the Muslims under Aragonese leadership and with the help of the Templars and the Order of St. John.

Located on the CidCT route: Camino del Cid Cyclotourista - Route of Castles in the Historically Contested Border Region between the Moors and the Kingdoms of Castile, Valencia, and Aragon.

Camino del Cid - Under the name "El Cid," Spain's knight Rodrigo Diaz de Vivar became a symbol of the Reconquista. The Castilian nobleman fought against Moorish rulers and Christian rivals in the 11th century. He never lost a single battle; the Moors respectfully called him Alsayyed (Lord of the Leaders), which later became "El Cid." The Spanish, however, always referred to him as "El Compeador" (The Warrior).

The Colonia de Santa Eulalia is an extraordinary historic settlement between Sax and Villena in the province of Alicante. Founded at the end of the 19th century by the noblemen Antonio de Padua Saavedra y Rodríguez de la Guerra and Mariano de Bertodano Roncalli, it is considered one of the first experiments in an agricultural-industrial community in Spain. The colonia was conceived as a self-sufficient village that combined work, living, and leisure. In addition to agricultural land for vineyards, olives, and almonds, factories, workshops, a palace, a theater, a casino, a church, and even its own train station were built.

Architecturally, the complex combines influences from the industrial and modernist styles and follows a clear, planned urban layout with two central squares and a main road connecting the entire complex. However, over the course of the 20th century, the colonia lost its economic importance, many buildings fell into disrepair, and today the site is partially abandoned. Despite its deterioration, the Colonia de Santa Eulalia was recognized as a Cultural Property of Special Interest (Bien de Interés Cultural) in 2016 and is thus protected. It is considered a fascinating example of an idealistic vision of social and economic community in 19th-century Spain.

An unreal place, time seems to have stood still a century ago. The buildings are half-ruined, yet very imposing. In addition to the workers' houses, a large flour and alcohol factory were built, as well as a theater, a manager's house, a casino, an inn, a train station, various wineries, warehouses, and oil mills. The buildings were organized around two squares, with the main square centered around the hermitage of Santa Eulalia. Some buildings and their functions are still recognizable.

The city of Villena, in the province of Alicante, is well worth a stop. Its old town has been declared a historic-artistic site and it is not surprising, since the town houses one of the most important Gothic ensembles in the Valencian Community, as well as a castle of Almohad origin. In addition, its archaeological museum contains the treasure of Villena, an impressive set of pieces of gold and other precious metals from the Bronze Age.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The Tour of Alto Vinalopó – Circular from Villena to Sax is approximately 31.0 km (19.3 miles) long. It can typically be completed in under two hours, with an estimated cycling duration of 1 hour and 53 minutes for most touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as 'easy' and is suitable for all skill levels and fitness levels. It features a modest elevation gain of around 130 meters (426 feet), indicating a relatively flat or gently undulating profile, making it ideal for recreational riders.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Alto Vinalopó a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route that starts and ends in Villena, offering a convenient loop for cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling tour in Villena?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, as the route starts and ends in the town of Villena, you should be able to find public parking within the town. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bikes and providing a comfortable ride. While there might be some minor inclines, the overall profile is relatively flat or gently undulating.

faqs.question: What scenery and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect to see a blend of arid terrain, fertile farming lands, and glimpses of the region's rich medieval heritage. Notable landmarks include the impressive Sax Fortress and the historical site of Colonia de Santa Eulalia. The route also connects the historic towns of Villena and Sax, both featuring prominent castles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Alto Vinalopó region?
faqs.answer: The Vinalopó region generally experiences a Mediterranean climate. For comfortable cycling, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are often ideal, offering milder temperatures compared to the heat of summer. Winter can also be pleasant, though cooler.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails. You will encounter parts of La Defensa del Sur (40%), Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana (31%), Camino de Santiago del Sureste (28%), and Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Alicante (20%), among others.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Alto Vinalopó – Circular from Villena to Sax dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a touring bicycle route, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists unless they can be safely carried in a bike trailer or basket. If you plan to walk sections, dogs should be kept on a leash, especially in populated areas or near roads.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public route between Villena and Sax.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Since the route connects the towns of Villena and Sax, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in both towns where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.
